sourceMovie.title: Vibes
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: People revisit Vibes for the same reason it broke through in 1988: clear stakes, memorable faces, and a action rhythm that doesn't stall in the middle. Ken Kwapis keeps the ensemble pointed in one direction. The romance and action blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: Love Potion No. 9
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Vibes clicked for you, Love Potion No. 9 (1992) is a strong next stop: both trade in romance and comedy, with comic timing and sharper banter. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Vibes clicked for you, What's Love Got to Do with It? (2023) is a strong next stop: both trade in romance and comedy, with comic timing and sharper banter. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after Vibes?
faq.answer: Try Love Potion No. 9, What's Love Got to Do with It?, Chéri—each shares DNA with Vibes while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Vibes?
faq.answer: Vibes (1988) is best described as Romance, Action, Comedy. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Vibes worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 5.7/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this romance lane.
